description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, dockerize, and deploy. In the build stage add a job named build that uses the ruby:2.3 image, runs before_script commands `apt-get update -yqqq`, `apt-get install -y nodejs`, and `bundle install --path vendor`, caches the vendor/ directory, executes `bundle exec middleman build`, and stores the resulting build/ directory as an artifact. In the dockerize stage add a job named dockerize that uses the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service, runs a before_script `docker login -u \"$CI_REGISTRY_USER\" -p \"$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD\" $CI_REGISTRY`, depends on the build job, and runs `docker build -t \"$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE\" .` followed by `docker push \"$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE\"`. In the deploy stage add a job named deploy that uses the sequenceiq/alpine-curl image, runs on runners tagged home and docker, defines variables CONTENT_TYPE and JSON_DATA, and executes `curl -X POST -H \"$CONTENT_TYPE\" -d \"$JSON_DATA\" \"$RANCHER_HOST\"` to trigger a Rancher deployment. No explicit rules are set, so jobs run on all branches.
